title: Specklinia fastigiata
taxonomy: Specklinia fastigiata belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a species within the genus Specklinia. This genus is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a group of small to medium-sized orchids. Specklinia fastigiata is native to South America and is often found in the Andes mountain range. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is widely recognized as part of this family and genus.

intro: Specklinia fastigiata, also known as the Yellow-speckled Specklinia, is a species of orchid found in Central and South America, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Specklinia fastigiata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
